message:
feat(demo): add mouse + keyboard shortcuts to Form Validation (bd-iuvb.17.11.6)

Add error injection (E), reset (R), clear errors (C) shortcuts,
mouse scroll to navigate fields, click error panel to toggle mode,
and 8 new tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1155,7 +1155,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.3","title":"Mermaid Showcase: mouse interactions for controls + diagram inspection","description":"Background
- MermaidShowcase already has rich keyboard controls, but mouse support should match expectations: click tabs, click samples, scroll panels.

Goal
- Add mouse-driven selection and inspection across the Mermaid showcase UI.

Mouse Interactions
- Click sample list entries.
- Wheel scrolls sample list and/or output pane depending on hover.
- If diagram supports hit regions: hover highlights node/edge; click selects.

Keyboard Shortcuts
- Ensure existing shortcuts are surfaced, and add a small number of high-signal ones (reset, toggle overlays, toggle glyph mode).

Acceptance Criteria
- Mouse-only exploration is viable for sample switching and scrolling.
- No regression in determinism/snapshots.

Coordination
- This task should coordinate with the Mermaid showcase epics (`bd-2nkmi`, `bd-309ob`) but focuses on UX wiring, not layout algorithms.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/mermaid_showcase.rs`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.308008795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.308008795Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mermaid","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.308008795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.308008795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.308008795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.308008795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.4","title":"Mermaid Mega Showcase: mouse-first control surface + diagram hit testing","description":"Background
- MermaidMegaShowcase is the over-the-top interactive lab screen. It should be *extremely* mouse-friendly.

Goal
- Ensure the mega screen's control surface (knobs, toggles, sample selection, overlays) is fully operable by mouse and has rich shortcuts.

Mouse Interactions
- Click any toggle/knob.
- Drag sliders where present.
- Wheel scrolls the control panel or diagram pane depending on hover.
- Diagram hit testing: hover highlights; click selects; drag pans (if supported).

Keyboard Shortcuts
- Add consistent keys for:
  - sample next/prev
  - toggle overlays
  - start/stop auto-run
  - reset state deterministically

Acceptance Criteria
- Mega screen feels like a control panel: nothing important is keyboard-only.
- Interactions do not regress performance; mouse move is coalesced.

Coordination
- Coordinate with the mega screen epic `bd-3oaig` (this task focuses on mouse/shortcut UX wiring).

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/mermaid_mega_showcase.rs`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.661205626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.661205626Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mega","mermaid","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.4","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.661205626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.4","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.661205626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.4","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.661205626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.4","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.661205626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","title":"Visual Effects: mouse controls for anchors/params + shortcut presets","description":"Background
- VisualEffects is a 'wow' screen. Mouse should control something immediate (anchor, look, parameter).

Goal
- Add obvious mouse controls (move/drag) and shortcuts for effect presets.

Mouse Interactions
- Mouse move updates an anchor or inspection point (already used in some harnesses).
- Drag adjusts one key parameter (e.g., intensity) with deterministic step.
- Wheel cycles effects or adjusts a focused parameter.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `N/P`: next/prev effect.
- `Space`: pause/resume animation.
- `R`: reset deterministic seed.

Acceptance Criteria
- Mouse interaction produces visible effect and is deterministic under fixed seed/time.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentFinch","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.973988659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:36:38.406121353Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:36:38.105733279Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts","vfx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.973988659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.973988659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.973988659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:41.973988659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":873,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SilentFinch verified: mouse handling, keybindings, hit-testing implemented. 160+ lib tests pass. Closing.","created_at":"2026-02-06T18:36:38Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.6","title":"Theme Studio: click/drag palette editing + shortcut toggles","description":"Background
- ThemeStudio should feel like a mini design tool.

Goal
- Make palette editing mouse-first (click swatches, drag sliders) with shortcuts for rapid toggles.

Mouse Interactions
- Click swatch selects color.
- Drag sliders adjusts channels deterministically.
- Wheel nudges selected channel or scrolls panel.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `Tab`: next field.
- `C`: copy/paste color (demo-local).
- `R`: reset to preset.

Acceptance Criteria
- Mouse-only palette edits are viable.
- Help overlay documents actions.

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.7","title":"Snapshot Player: mouse scrubbing + click-to-jump + shortcuts","description":"Background
- SnapshotPlayer (Time Travel Studio) is one of the 'best stuff' screens.

Goal
- Make time travel controls mouse-first: click to jump, drag to scrub, wheel to step.

Mouse Interactions
- Click timeline to jump to frame.
- Drag timeline thumb to scrub.
- Wheel steps frame-by-frame when hovered.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `Space`: play/pause.
- `←/→`: prev/next frame.
- `A/B`: toggle comparison modes.
- `R`: reset session.

Acceptance Criteria
- Scrubbing is deterministic and does not flicker.
- Snapshot tests cover a non-zero frame selection state.

Key References

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.11","title":"Mouse/Shortcuts Pass: Interaction screens","description":"Purpose
- Track mouse-first + shortcut-rich upgrades for Interaction-category screens.

Included Screens
- Forms & Input
- File Browser
- Macro Recorder
- Notifications
- Mouse Playground
- Form Validation
- Command Palette Evidence Lab
- Hyperlink Playground
- Kanban Board

Interaction Themes
- Click-to-focus and click-to-activate are primary.
- Wheel scrolls lists/panes under pointer.
- Drag-drop and selection gestures work where expected.
- Keyboard shortcuts still offer full control for non-mouse users.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:55.221357072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:55.221357072Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","interaction","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:55.221357072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.1","title":"Forms & Input: click-to-focus fields + mouse wheel + shortcuts","description":"Background
- Forms are one of the most common interaction patterns. This screen should feel like a real UI.

Goal
- Make all controls operable with mouse and provide shortcuts for power navigation.

Mouse Interactions
- Click to focus a field.
- Click toggles and buttons to activate.
- Wheel scrolls the form when content overflows.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `Tab`/`Shift+Tab`: next/prev control.
- `Enter`: activate primary action.
- `Ctrl+R`: reset form state.

Acceptance Criteria
- Focus is consistent between mouse and keyboard.
- Help overlay lists interaction keys.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/forms_input.rs`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentFinch","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:50:32.921900055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:33:29.870362160Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:33:29.566463150Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","forms","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:50:32.921900055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:50:32.921900055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:50:32.921900055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:50:32.921900055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":864,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SilentFinch verified: mouse handling, keybindings, hit-testing all implemented. Tests pass. Closing.","created_at":"2026-02-06T18:33:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","title":"Mouse/Shortcuts Pass: Tour screens","description":"Purpose
- Track and drive the mouse-first + shortcut-rich upgrades for the demo's Tour-category experiences.

Included Screens (current)
- Guided Tour (base exists; v2 work tracked elsewhere in bd-iuvb.17.6)
- Dashboard
- Inline Mode Story (scrollback-first constraints)

Interaction Goals (Tour)
- Mouse-first navigation and 'clickable tiles' where the UI suggests it.
- Clear, cinematic affordances: hover highlight, click to drill in, wheel scroll where content overflows.
- Shortcuts that demonstrate capabilities quickly (toggle overlays, switch tiles, trigger scripted sequences).

Definition of Done
- Each included screen has at least 1 meaningful mouse interaction and at least 3-5 meaningful shortcuts.
- Help overlay entries are accurate and tested.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"HazyRaven","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:45:48.169248562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:34:20.734837655Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts","tour"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:45:48.169248562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","title":"Dashboard: clickable tiles + mouse routing + power shortcuts","description":"Background
- Dashboard is the demo's cinematic overview. It already lays out multiple 'tiles' that visually read as clickable panes.
- The dashboard view currently registers pane hit regions for several tiles (see `dashboard.rs` registering `chrome::register_pane_hit`), but global mouse routing does not yet use those hits.

Goal
- Make the dashboard feel like a real clickable home screen.

Mouse Interactions
- Hover highlights the tile under the pointer.
- Click a tile to navigate to the corresponding screen.
- Scroll wheel over a tile that has scrollable content scrolls that tile (or shows a hint that it is not scrollable).

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `Enter`: activate currently highlighted tile.
- `1-9`: jump to specific tiles in a stable order (documented).
- `R`: refresh/reseed any animated tiles deterministically (if used).

Acceptance Criteria
- Clicking any dashboard tile navigates deterministically and stops the guided tour if active.
- Help overlay lists dashboard shortcuts.
- Snapshot test covers hover highlight + active tile.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/dashboard.rs` (tile layout + pane hit registration)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (mouse routing)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkRidge","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T09:13:00.386710034Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","title":"Inline Mode Story: mouse-friendly without scrollback regression","description":"Background
- Inline Mode is a core differentiator: preserve native terminal scrollback while keeping chrome stable.
- Mouse capture can easily break this by stealing the scroll wheel; this screen must be the 'golden example' of doing it right.

Goal
- Make the Inline Mode story screen interactive and mouse-friendly *while keeping scrollback preservation obvious and intact*.

Mouse Interactions
- Default (mouse mode OFF): wheel scrolls terminal scrollback; screen should explicitly tell the user that this is intentional.
- Clickable elements inside the inline UI:
  - Click on highlighted callouts to jump to the next/previous story beat.
  - Click on 'Try it' prompts to trigger a deterministic mini-action (e.g., show/hide chrome, toggle a marker line).
- Optional opt-in: allow toggling mouse mode ON for this screen with explicit warning that wheel now scrolls UI.

Keyboard Shortcuts
- Keys to step the story forward/back, toggle example modes, and toggle mouse mode.
- Include a one-key 'reset story' action that restores the initial state deterministically.

Acceptance Criteria
- Inline scrollback is preserved by default (wheel scrolls terminal scrollback).
- Any opt-in mouse mode change is explicit and discoverable.
- Screen is still demonstrably interactive (click + shortcuts) even in default scrollback-preserving mode.
- Help overlay accurately describes the interaction contract and tradeoffs.

Tests / Validation
- PTY E2E: Inline mode run asserts mouse capture is not enabled by default.
- Snapshot test covers the explanatory copy and the interactive affordances.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/inline_mode_story.rs`
- `crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:13:55.281312533Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:13:55.281293187Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: inline scrollback preserved by default (PTY E2E test confirms mouse capture off), 8 keyboard shortcuts handled (Space/A/C/D/H/M/R/T) plus mouse clicks and wheel, reset-to-defaults shortcut (D) added, 10 help entries documenting interaction contract, 8 unit tests + 2 snapshot tests at 80x24 and 120x40 pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","inline-mode","mouse","scrollback","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-iuvb.17.9","title":"Mouse/Shortcuts Pass: Core screens","description":"Purpose
- Track mouse-first + shortcut-rich upgrades for Core-category screens.

Included Screens

- Layout Lab
- Advanced Features
- Responsive Demo
- Intrinsic Sizing
- Layout Inspector

Core Interaction Themes
- Clickable controls and panels behave like real UI (hover, click, wheel scroll where appropriate).

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.1","title":"Widget Gallery: click-to-focus + mouse scrolling + shortcut power","description":"Background
- Widget Gallery is the canonical catalog of widgets. It should feel like a real interactive component library.

Goal
- Make the gallery strongly mouse-friendly and give it shortcuts that let users rapidly explore widget behavior.

Mouse Interactions
- Hover highlights the widget card/region under pointer.
- Click selects/focuses a widget region and shows an interaction hint (what wheel/keys do).
- Wheel scrolls within the active widget region (list/table/tree) when applicable.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `J/K` or `↑/↓`: next/prev widget section.
- `Enter`: activate focused widget (e.g., toggle a switch / open a modal sample).
- `T`: cycle widget themes/presets.
- `F`: toggle a compact filter/search input for widget list.
- `R`: reset demo state deterministically.

Acceptance Criteria
- Mouse-only exploration is viable: a user can click around and see state changes.
- At least 5 meaningful shortcuts are available and listed in help overlay.
- Snapshot tests cover at least one focused/hovered state.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/widget_gallery.rs`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentFinch","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:48:47.464152779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:36:40.231606994Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:36:39.953210242Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts","widget-gallery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:48:47.464152779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:48:47.464152779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:48:47.464152779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:48:47.464152779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":875,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SilentFinch verified: mouse handling, keybindings, hit-testing implemented. 160+ lib tests pass. Closing.","created_at":"2026-02-06T18:36:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.2","title":"Layout Lab: mouse-driven constraint manipulation + rich shortcuts","description":"Background
- Layout Lab is the 'constraints in motion' demo.

Goal
- Make layout experiments directly manipulable with the mouse (drag handles, click to select constraints) and provide shortcuts for fast exploration.

Mouse Interactions
- Click a panel/box to select it.
- Drag handles to adjust size constraints or weights (deterministic steps).
- Wheel over selected element adjusts a primary parameter (spacing/weight) with a visible readout.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `H`: toggle layout overlays (bounds, constraints, guides).
- `Space`: play/pause any layout animation.
- `[`/`]`: decrease/increase spacing.
- `R`: reset scenario.

Acceptance Criteria
- Mouse drag changes layout deterministically and is reversible/resettable.
- Help overlay documents mouse and keyboard interactions.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/layout_lab.rs`
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/layout_inspector.rs`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:48:47.752236233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:59:37.166729656Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:59:37.166711111Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","layout","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:48:47.752236233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:48:47.752236233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:48:47.752236233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:48:47.752236233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.3","title":"Advanced Features: make every panel clickable + shortcut-driven","description":"Background
- Advanced Features is a grab-bag of composite patterns (timers, spinners, diagnostics).

Goal
- Turn passive examples into interactive ones: click to toggle, click to drill in, shortcuts to trigger scenarios.

Mouse Interactions
- Click toggles for each feature module.
- Wheel scrolls within any log/diagnostic pane.

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `S`: start/stop spinner demo.
- `D`: toggle diagnostics verbosity.
- `R`: reset.

Acceptance Criteria
- At least one mouse interaction per sub-panel.
- At least 3-5 meaningful shortcuts.

Key References
